sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: NavigationHeader is always eligible and required as the first section. For Explorer archetype at the decision stage, the full variant provides wayfinding context that lets trial users orient themselves and access deeper content, while the persistent CTA in nav serves as a constant conversion touchpoint for users ready to upgrade.

sections.name: Hero — Value Proposition Restatement
sections.rationale: Explorer archetype trial users at the decision stage need a compelling opening that reframes the product's value in terms of what they've already experienced. HeroStatement suits Explorers by allowing longer body copy that makes the argument for upgrading without aggressive CTA pressure — ideal for users who want to feel intellectually convinced rather than pushed. FeatureGrid in 2-col-heavy Explorer variant could alternatively open with a capability-led narrative.

sections.name: Capability Deep-Dive
sections.rationale: Explorer archetype users at the decision stage need structured, thorough articulation of what they gain by converting to paid. FeatureGrid in the Explorer 2-col-heavy variant provides the longer copy per feature that Explorers expect, allowing benefit statements that map trial limitations to paid capabilities. This builds the rational case for upgrade at the decision funnel stage.

sections.name: Mid-Page Trust Signal — Testimonial
sections.rationale: TestimonialSingle is eligible for the decision funnel stage. While its archetype is Validator, placing a single credible testimonial here serves the Explorer's need for evidence-based decision-making at the decision stage. With role, company, and photo attribution, it provides the depth of proof an Explorer requires before committing. Positioned after capability articulation, it validates the argument just made.

sections.name: Pricing Comparison
sections.rationale: PricingCard targets the decision funnel stage and gives Explorer-minded trial users the concrete information they need to evaluate tiers. The with-comparison variant supports the Explorer's desire to thoroughly understand feature differentiation before upgrading. Placing pricing after trust and capability sections ensures the Explorer has built sufficient context to interpret plans meaningfully at this decision moment.

sections.name: Final Conversion Prompt
sections.rationale: CTABanner at the decision stage serves as the terminal action capture for Explorer trial users who have now moved through the full narrative arc. The Validator variant with subhead and trust signal ('Cancel anytime', 'No long-term contract') addresses the Explorer's residual hesitation with reassurance, converting their thorough evaluation into action at the decision-to-conversion threshold.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal is always eligible and required as the final section. The with-cta variant is critical here for Explorer archetype users at the decision stage — Explorers who scroll to the bottom without converting are still engaged and evaluating; a recovery CTA recaptures these deliberate, thorough users at their final decision moment.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.
Explorer variant uses longer body copy and suppresses primary CTA pressure, allowing trial users in the decision stage to absorb the narrative without aggressive conversion tactics.

slots.eyebrow: You've seen what MODE can do
slots.headline: Now build without the limits holding you back
slots.subhead: Your trial gave you a taste of intent-aware design — upgrading unlocks the full system your team needs to ship faster and smarter.
slots.body: During your trial you've been working with a subset of what MODE offers. The components, the logic, the adaptive behaviour — it all scales significantly when you move to a paid plan. Teams that upgrade typically unlock capabilities that change how they scope and deliver product work, not just how they style it. This is where MODE shifts from a tool you're evaluating to a system you're building on.

page.reasoning: Explorer variant uses 2-col-heavy with longer copy per feature, enabling Explorers to deeply understand product capabilities during their decision-making phase.

slots.headline: What changes when you move to paid
slots.subhead: Six capabilities that trial users consistently cite as the reason they upgraded — and stayed.

features.title: Full component library access
features.description: The trial surfaces a curated subset of MODE's component system. Pro unlocks every pattern, variant, and composition — including intent-aware states that adapt automatically to funnel stage, archetype, and context mode. Teams stop rebuilding the same components from scratch and start shipping coherent interfaces in a fraction of the time.

features.title: Analytics-connected design decisions
features.description: Move beyond static layouts with live performance data wired directly into your design system. Pro users can see which component variants are converting, where drop-off occurs, and which intent signals are being triggered — all without leaving the MODE environment. Design decisions become evidence-backed rather than instinct-led.

features.title: Intent engine at full resolution
features.description: Trial mode runs MODE's intent engine at partial sensitivity, limiting the number of context signals it can act on simultaneously. The full engine reads up to 14 distinct intent signals per session, dynamically adjusting layouts, copy slots, and CTA prominence in real time. The difference in conversion performance is measurable from the first week.

features.title: Team permissions and version control
features.description: Trial accounts are single-user by design. Pro introduces role-based permissions, branching, and a full audit history so entire product and design teams can collaborate on a shared system without stepping on each other's work. Every change is attributed, reversible, and reviewable — reducing friction in cross-functional handoffs significantly.

features.title: Custom token and theming system
features.description: Pro users gain access to MODE's token architecture, allowing deep brand customisation across every component without overriding the underlying logic. Set your colour system, typography scale, spacing model, and motion values once — and watch them propagate consistently across every surface your team touches. Your design language, powered by MODE's intelligence.

features.title: Priority support and onboarding
features.description: When you upgrade, you don't just get more features — you get a team behind you. Pro includes access to a dedicated onboarding specialist, a private Slack channel, and a response SLA under nine minutes during business hours. Teams report feeling fully productive with MODE an average of 2.3x faster than those who self-onboard through documentation alone.

page.reasoning: with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity, and testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment — critical for trial users moving toward conversion.

slots.quote: We spent two weeks in the MODE trial convinced we were getting most of the value — then we upgraded and realised we'd been seeing about 40% of what it could do. The intent engine alone changed how we think about structuring our onboarding funnel. Within six weeks of going Pro, our trial-to-paid conversion rate was up 31%. I wish we'd made the decision earlier.

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list; Explorer users benefit from fuller context when comparing paid plans during decision stage.

slots.description: Everything a serious product team needs to design, ship, and optimise intent-aware SaaS interfaces — without stitching together separate tools.
features: Full component library — all variants and intent states
Intent engine at full resolution (14 signals)
Unlimited team seats with role-based permissions
Priority support with < 9 min response SLA

page.reasoning: split for section-end with directional copy on left, CTA on right — provides narrative context for Explorers while maintaining clear conversion action at decision moment.

slots.headline: You've done the research. MODE Pro is ready when you are.
slots.subhead: Join over 4,800 product teams who converted their trial after seeing exactly what you've seen on this page.
slots.trust_signal: Cancel anytime. No long-term contract. Your data and configuration carry over instantly.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows, essential for converting trial users at the decision stage.
